# Per-minute failed-logon burst detection with bin time-bucketing + tiered classification (T1110)

**Dataset**: `gx_05_auth`

Detects rapid failed-logon bursts that a per-user total would smear out, by bucketing events into one-minute windows and counting failures per user per window. A slow trickle of failures over a day looks the same as a 4-in-one-minute spray-and-pray unless you bin on time first. Note `bin` is a standalone stage (`bin ts span = 1m`) that must run before the `comp ... by user, ts` so each window aggregates independently; the `if()` then tiers each window as SPIKE / elevated / low.

```sql
dataset = gx_05_auth
| filter outcome = "failure"
| bin ts span = 1m
| comp count() as fail_burst, count_distinct(src_ip) as ips, values(host) as hosts_in_window by user, ts
| alter burst_flag = if(fail_burst >= 4, "SPIKE", fail_burst >= 2, "elevated", "low")
| filter fail_burst >= 2
| sort desc fail_burst
```
